channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI analyzes Apple's deliberate approach to AI development and suggests their measured strategy is proving beneficial. The article questions whether Apple's recent AI initiatives address concerns about the company lagging in the competitive AI race.

item.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer channel announces a World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. The announcement includes ticketing and scheduling information available at ai.engineer/wf.

item.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Brendan Foody of Mercor accuses Sequoia Capital of using dual-pricing tactics to assign different valuations to identical equity stakes. The allegation highlights a practice Foody claims is common among top-tier venture capital firms.

item.title: Siri AI at WWDC 2026
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison comments on Apple's June 2026 Siri AI announcements, noting that while the new features appear technically feasible using vision LLMs and Gemini-derived models run on Private Cloud Compute, past failures justify a "show me" attitude. The new Core AI library enables developers to run PyTorch models on Apple hardware via Core AI PyTorch Extensions.

item.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly laying off staff due to struggles generating revenue. This news emerges as OpenAI files for IPO.

item.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports that Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote featured AI product demonstrations that seemed more authentic following a $250 million settlement for false advertising claims. The article notes subtle shifts in demo presentation, such as realistic usage scenarios involving people with phones in hand.

item.title: Ask HN: How do you cope when your startup contracts?
item.description: jasonephraim: A technical lead asks how to cope with repeated startup contractions where they absorb work from layoffs, face increased scrutiny from management, and struggle with role ambiguity. They question whether their startup choices are vulnerable to VC trends and seek guidance on navigating career risk, job market conditions, and the pattern of overextension.

item.title: They Spent Years on a Math Problem. Then They Were Scooped by A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: A New York Times article discusses how researchers who spent years working on a mathematical problem were beaten to the solution by an AI system. The story highlights the competitive pressure and evolving landscape of research as AI capabilities advance in solving complex mathematical challenges.

item.title: Show HN: Command Center, the AI coding env for people who care about quality
item.description: Darmani: Command Center is an agentic coding environment that focuses on code quality by helping developers read, refactor, and understand AI-generated code. It provides features like code refactoring, visual walkthroughs of large diffs, inline code editing through agent commands, and integration with existing editors like Zed.

item.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO, following Anthropic's similar filing about a week earlier. The move signals an escalating competitive dynamic between the two leading AI companies.

item.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO
item.description: rvz: OpenAI has confidentially filed for an initial public offering. This signals intent to become a public company, marking a significant corporate milestone for the AI research organization.

item.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
item.description: jbernardo95: Apple is pursuing a strategy to attract smaller developers by offering more affordable AI capabilities. The move signals a competitive positioning aimed at democratizing AI access across developer tiers.

item.title: AI and Agency
item.description: demaree: Post titled "AI and Agency" discussing the relationship between AI systems and human or autonomous agency. Details are limited but the topic suggests philosophical or practical commentary on AI capabilities and autonomy.

item.title: Apple Reveals New A.I.-Powered Version of Its Siri Digital Assistant - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Apple has revealed a new AI-powered version of its Siri digital assistant. The update was announced at WWDC 2026 and represents a significant makeover to the assistant's capabilities.

item.title: Show HN: macOS Apps on Linux: SwiftUI for Linux (and AppKit, NSFoundation, etc.)
item.description: ljlolel: QuillUI is an open-source project that allows existing macOS Swift projects to compile for Linux using GTK or Qt backends. It implements SwiftUI, AppKit, and NSFoundation for Linux, enabling cross-platform deployment of native Swift applications across macOS and Linux.

item.title: Why LLM Inference Needs a New Kind of Router
item.description: aviziva: Author argues that LLM inference workloads require a different routing approach than standard network routers. This reflects debates around how to efficiently distribute and manage inference requests across heterogeneous infrastructure.

item.title: The Third Generation of Apple's Foundation Models
item.description: 2bit: Apple has developed a third generation of foundation models. The announcement discusses Apple's progress in building its own foundation model capabilities, likely for on-device and intelligence features.

item.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
item.description: unclefuzzy: Apple has revealed a new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models. This represents a partnership between Apple and Google for AI infrastructure.

item.title: Apple Core AI Framework
item.description: hmokiguess: Apple Core AI Framework is a framework for AI development released by Apple. It provides infrastructure for building and deploying AI models.

item.title: Microsoft Hacked to Deliver Malware to Claude and Gemini Users
item.description: guessmyname: A Hacker News post reports that Microsoft was compromised to deliver malware targeting Claude and Gemini users. This describes a purported supply-chain security incident involving major cloud platforms and AI assistants.

item.title: Ask HN: What are tools you have made for yourself since the advent of AI?
item.description: aryamaan: A Hacker News discussion thread asking community members to share the tools they have built for themselves in response to the emergence of advanced AI. The question explores personal technical responses and applications of AI technology.

item.title: Train Models Faster with JAX and MaxText Using NVFP4 on NVIDIA Blackwell
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA Developer blog post presenting training efficiency improvements using JAX and MaxText frameworks with NVFP4 precision format on NVIDIA Blackwell accelerators. The post focuses on throughput optimization for frontier LLM pre-training across large-scale distributed systems.

item.title: Siri AI
item.description: 0xedb: Siri AI appears to be a discussion or update related to Apple's Siri voice assistant. The specific technical details or context are not provided in the minimal source material.

item.title: Latent Spatial Memory for Video World Models
item.description: Weijie Wang, Haoyu Zhao, Yifan Yang: Latent spatial memory is a persistent 3D cache that stores scene information directly in diffusion latent space to improve video world models. The proposed Mirage framework lifts latent tokens into 3D via depth-guided back-projection and queries memory through direct latent-space warping, achieving 10.57x faster generation and 55x memory reduction compared to explicit 3D baselines.

item.title: MemoryVLA++: Temporal Modeling via Memory and Imagination in Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description: Hao Shi, Weiye Li, Bin Xie: MemoryVLA++ is a temporal modeling framework for vision-language-action models in robotic manipulation that integrates working memory via a Perceptual-Cognitive Memory Bank, future imagination via a world model, and action generation via diffusion. The system encodes observations into perceptual and cognitive tokens, retrieves relevant historical context, imagines future states in latent space, and generates temporally consistent action sequences, achieving substantial gains on long-horizon and

item.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
item.description: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu: OmniGameArena is a unified UE5 benchmark for evaluating vision-language model agents across twelve newly built games spanning Solo, PvP, and Coop modes. It introduces the Improvement Dynamics Curve, a reflection harness that tracks how VLM agent performance evolves across multiple rounds of autonomous skill prompt refinement.

item.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
item.description: Anton Bolychev, Georgiy Malaniya, Sinan Ibrahim: An agency-transferring method improves RL policy training efficiency by progressively shifting control from a suboptimal baseline policy to a trainable learning policy. The method maintains high goal-reaching rates throughout training and produces a standalone neural network policy that eventually operates without baseline support.

item.title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
item.description: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda: The paper introduces a causal evaluation framework using probabilistic finite automata and a binning semiring to study how task-specific data frequency affects language model learnability. It demonstrates that standard correlational evaluation practices yield flawed conclusions and proposes decomposed KL-divergence metrics to measure sub-task learnability causally.

item.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
item.description: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi: DRPO replaces hard divergence masks with smooth advantage-weighted quadratic regularization on policy shift to stabilize LLM RL training. This approach preserves trust-region geometry while providing bounded, continuous gradient weights that correct diverging updates beyond trust-region boundaries.

item.title: Weighted universal approximation of differentiable maps on infinite-dimensional manifolds
item.description: Philipp Schmocker, Josef Teichmann: The paper generalizes the universal approximation theorem for functional input neural networks (FNNs) to differentiable maps by proving approximation of derivatives on infinite-dimensional weighted manifolds. A weighted Nachbin theorem is established to show that FNNs can approximate both functions and their derivatives, with applications to path space functionals and non-anticipative functionals.

item.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
item.description: Danqi Zhuang, Jisui Huang, Xiaoyue Xi: PTL-Diffusion extends standard diffusion models by replacing a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution with a periodic family of Gaussian laws in the forward process. The method embeds phase structure directly into forward noising dynamics via a periodically forced Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process, enabling better manifold-aware generation with closed-form marginals and Gaussian reverse posteriors.

item.title: iMaC: Translating Actions into Motion and Contact Images for Embodied World Models
item.description: Zhenyu Wu, Xiuwei Xu, Yukun Zhou: iMac proposes treating raw visual images as native action representations for embodied world models, replacing low-dimensional structured action vectors. The approach uses a dual-branch architecture with an image-action encoder and dynamic world predictor to achieve high-fidelity future state prediction and closed-loop embodied control. Experiments show improvements in prediction accuracy, task success rate, and cross-scene generalization over vector-based baselines.

item.title: Nex N2 Pro: Frontier agentic performance at 400B
item.description: himata4113: Nex N2 Pro is a 400 billion parameter language model designed for agentic performance. It represents frontier-level capabilities in autonomous agent applications.

item.title: AHA-WAM:Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing
item.description: Jisong Cai, Long Ling, Shiwei Chu: AHA-WAM is an asynchronous world-action modeling approach that decouples world prediction and action execution at different temporal resolutions using dual Diffusion Transformers. The video branch models low-frequency long-horizon scene dynamics while the action branch executes high-frequency short action chunks, coordinated through observation-guided context routing and horizon-adaptive offset training. The method achieves 92.8% success on RoboTwin and 78.3% on real-world manipulation tasks wit

item.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
item.description: Avijit Ghosh, Anka Reuel, Jenny Chim: Evaluation Cards is a reporting framework that standardizes and composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a unified record. It implements four interpretive signals (reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance and risk, score comparability) with reader modes for different stakeholders. The authors deployed this monitoring tool across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results.

item.title: Topological Neural Operators
item.description: Lennart Bastian, Samuel Leventhal, Mustafa Hajij: Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) extend neural operators from point/edge functions to topological domains by representing data as features on cells of varying dimension and modeling interactions through Discrete Exterior Calculus. Hierarchical TNOs (HTNOs) incorporate learned coarse complexes for long-range information propagation. The framework unifies operator learning across discretizations and improves accuracy on PDE benchmarks including irregular-geometry flow problems.

item.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
item.description: Wayne King, Zeyue Xue, Yuxuan Bian: Echo-Memory is a controlled study isolating memory mechanisms in action-conditioned video world models. It compares memory designs including raw context, compression-based memory, spatial summaries, and state-space recurrence under fixed backbones and evaluation protocols. The work introduces a three-branch evaluation protocol (replay quality, in-domain loop revisit, open-domain return) and finds that raw context capacity and state-space recurrence outperform aggressive compression for rememberi

item.title: Bandits for Efficient Experimentation: Adapting to Control Group, Preferences, and Context Drifts
item.description: Udvas Das, Waris Radji, Debabrota Basu: The paper introduces Dri-MED, an algorithm for linear contextual bandits with personalized user preferences and drifting context distributions over time. It adapts the MED strategy to handle heteroskedastic non-stationary noise and enforces baseline reward constraints, achieving instance-dependent regret scaling and low constraint violations in practice.

item.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
item.description: Shizhe Lin, Ladan Tahvildari: FASE (Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy) approximates functional correctness in code generation using minimum spanning trees of structural and semantic dissimilarity graphs. It outperforms existing semantic entropy methods while reducing computational cost to approximately 0.3% of traditional approaches. The method is evaluated on HumanEval and BigCodeBench benchmarks.

item.title: Beyond Spherical Harmonics: Rethinking Appearance Models for Radiance Reconstruction
item.description: Ewa Miazga, Jorge Condor, Piotr Didyk: The paper proposes Normalized Anisotropic Spherical Gabor functions as an alternative to spherical harmonics for view-dependent appearance modeling in novel-view synthesis. The approach achieves higher-quality reconstruction of high-frequency effects like specular reflections and glints while being significantly more memory-efficient than existing methods.

item.title: End-to-End Optimization of Incoherent Imaging for Classification Under Detector-Limited Readout
item.description: Archer Wang, Joshua Chen, Sachin Vaidya: This paper studies end-to-end optimization of phase masks and neural networks for incoherent imaging classification. It establishes when joint optical-computational design beats conventional lenses, finding gains arise mainly under detector readout constraints and proving conventional lenses approach mutual-information ceilings under full readout.

item.title: POTATR: A Lightweight Image-to-Graph Model for Page-Level Table Extraction
item.description: Brandon Smock, Libin Liang, Max Sokolov: POTATR is a 29M parameter image-to-graph model for page-level table extraction from documents. It extends Table Transformer and outperforms larger MLLMs on PubTables-v2 while running 130x faster and at 300x lower cost, with spatially grounded outputs enabling visual verification.

item.title: Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration: Automating Time-Series Models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum
item.description: Abd Elghani Meliani, Arora Sagar, Adlen Ksentini: The paper proposes Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration, an automated framework for time-series forecasting in Cloud-Edge Continuum environments. It addresses the cold start problem by merging sparse local telemetry data with TimeTrack, a high-resolution public dataset, then applying Neural Architecture Search to generate accurate baseline models.

item.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
item.description: Yifan Wang: The paper presents Intervention-Aware Variational Quantum Differentiable Predictive Control (IA-VQC-DPC), which trains quantum circuit policies under intervention budgets that penalize reliance on safety filters and evaluates them with a safety-attribution protocol. The approach decomposes executed trajectory corrections to measure whether safety is earned by the policy or its protective layers rather than masking incompetence.

item.title: HN seems dead compared to say 10-15 years ago
item.description: morpheos137: The author argues that Hacker News has declined in value compared to a decade ago, citing reduced originality, nostalgia-driven content, and excessive LLM-based projects lacking genuine innovation. The post raises questions about sustainable growth in an era of constrained resources.

item.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
item.description: Matthew Ho, Brian Liu, Jixuan Chen: SIGA is a Simulator-Interface Grounding Adapter that enables off-the-shelf coding agents to operate specialized scientific simulation software by supplying domain-specific contracts through retrieval, procedural memory, validation, and self-evolution. Evaluated on GEOS, SIGA produces complete simulator configurations 36x faster than expert humans while self-improvement mechanisms achieve 10% relative gains on held-out tasks.

item.title: SemDINO: A DINOv3-Driven Network for Cross-Temporal Semantic Alignment in Change Detection
item.description: Xinyu Tong, Meihua Zhou, Jinxiao Sun: SemDINO is an end-to-end semantic change detection network that combines DINOv3 features with CNN backbones via gated pyramid fusion and employs multi-scale temporal bidirectional transformer interaction for cross-temporal alignment. It incorporates semantic purification and change enhancement modules to suppress pseudo-changes and achieves state-of-the-art performance on remote sensing change detection benchmarks.

item.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
item.description: Badr AlKhamissi, Johannes Mehrer, Lara Marinov: Topo-Omni is a topographic multimodal model that learns shared spatial organization across visual, auditory, and language processing by fine-tuning a pretrained foundation model with a spatial smoothness objective. The resulting single contiguous in-silico cortical sheet develops functionally selective clusters consistent with human neuroimaging and can predict novel functional areas validated in human brain data.

item.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
item.description: Alexander Chulzhanov, Soeren Eberhardt, Arjun Mukherjee: The paper demonstrates data synthesis from community dictionaries to bootstrap neural machine translation for Q'eqchi' Mayan without web scraping. Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning via LoRA adapters on mT5-base achieves high structural BLEU on synthetic data but shows limited semantic grounding on organic text, revealing overfitting to synthetic templates and suggesting that authentic data is necessary for semantic refinement.

item.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
item.description: Lawrence Keunho Jang, Mareks Woodside, Geronimo Carom: iOSWorld is an interactive native iOS simulator benchmark with 26 apps, persistent user identity, and 133 tasks spanning single-app, multi-app, and personalization scenarios. It evaluates frontier and open-source computer-use models in vision-only and vision+XML settings, with the best model reaching 52% overall accuracy.
